author: potuz
content:
Hello I want to bring variable PTC deadlines again. I think this was ditched quickly and with no good reason. I think the following works absolutely fine: 

- builder adds the compressed size to the message and signs over it. 
- gossip ignores messages that don't match the compressed size. 


It's irrelevant if different clients use different compression. And the signing makes it impossible to rebroadcast cause it's more expensive than the fast ignore. 

I am not advocating for having this at the fork. But why wouldn't the above work? Cc <@654267572107083777> that had complains about enshrining compression algorithm. I think that's irrelevant here

author: tersec
content: Yeah, compressed size of the message per se is fair to include. It's specifically assuming the compression algorithm, and this doesn't do that. But compressed size is intrinsically related to the gossip network artifacts

author: tersec
content: or, well

author: tersec
content: it depends

author: tersec
content: It means clients would have to relay the exact byte message on the network, but that might be fine

author: potuz
content: It doesn't assume the algo

author: tersec
content: sorry, phrased that badly, it's specifically assuming the compression algorithm that's a problem

author: potuz
content: This is true, I'm assuming clients do this already

author: tersec
content: likely, yes, because of how libp2p works

author: tersec
content: and it's just inefficient to recompress

author: potuz
content: Also it would break all assumptions about message id with hashes otherwise

author: tersec
content: yes

author: tersec
content: so this seems safe enough

author: potuz
content: Unfortunately this makes the feature a hard fork though

author: tersec
content: ok, processwise my concern is that due to the headliner/nonheadliner distinction if ePBS keeps growing in scope, it blocks out nonheadliners. already, almost no nonheadliners (8061 excepted for example) on CL have gone in

author: tersec
content: this isn't per-se an ePBS issue, it's how the get-ePBS-stable-first approach works. If ePBS changes could be interleaved more with nonheadliners it would alleviate this

author: tersec
content: but yeah if this isn't aimed at Gloas but Heze there's plenty of time
